M-066.NAME: vpn-config-download-mime-hardening
M-066.owns: HTTP response semantics and frontend download Blob semantics for WireGuard and AmneziaVPN .conf files.
M-066.purpose: Prevent Android, iOS, and desktop browsers from renaming VPN configuration downloads from .conf to .conf.txt by forcing safe attachment and MIME headers.

exports.fn-build-config-download-response: Return config bytes with attachment, application/octet-stream, filename, filename-star, nosniff, and private no-store headers.
exports.fn-sanitize-config-download-filename: Build ASCII-safe .conf filenames without path separators, duplicate extensions, Unicode surprises, or .txt suffixes.
exports.fn-download-vpn-config: Preserve the authenticated /api/v1/vpn/config/download contract while changing only download response headers and body encoding.
exports.ui-download-config-blob: Create browser download Blobs with application/octet-stream and a .conf download attribute for cached or managed device configs.

constraints.constraint-1: Do not change install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, TLS, VPN profile generation, AWG/WG key material, or existing config text content in Phase-48.
constraints.constraint-2: The download endpoint must stay authenticated and must not expose configs through public URLs.
constraints.constraint-3: Content-Type must not be text/plain for attachment downloads because Android Chrome may append .txt.
constraints.constraint-4: Filename must be ASCII-safe, deterministic, and end with exactly .conf.
constraints.constraint-5: QR, copy-to-clipboard, raw config display, and JSON config retrieval must keep their current behavior unless explicitly changed in a later phase.

acceptance-metrics.metric-1: /api/v1/vpn/config/download returns application/octet-stream, attachment, filename .conf, filename-star .conf, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, and Cache-Control no-store/private.
acceptance-metrics.metric-2: Response body remains byte-for-byte equivalent to the generated VPN config text after UTF-8 encoding.
acceptance-metrics.metric-3: Frontend-created download Blobs use application/octet-stream and keep download filenames ending in .conf.
acceptance-metrics.metric-4: No server or frontend path emits .conf.txt, text/plain, or unsafe path-derived filenames for config downloads.
acceptance-metrics.metric-5: Backend router tests, frontend build, Phase-48 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and deploy/install diff guard pass.

M-066.hotspot: H-053: Mobile browsers may rename .conf downloads to .txt unless the backend and frontend force attachment, octet-stream, filename, and nosniff semantics
